---
title: "LRP84: Period-resolved dose-response — intervention dose -> phoneme blending (B)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP84 is the phoneme-blending companion to [`lrp-rli-dose-077`](../lrp-rli-dose-077/) (word reading), completing the dose-response family's coverage of the two largest ITT effects (`L` and `B`). The estimand, causal structure and caveats are identical to LRP77; only the outcome changes.

**Estimand.** How blending _conditional change_ relates to the per-period intervention **dose** (`attend`), with partial pooling across the three periods and a test of whether the dose–gain slope **varies by period**. The outcome is the Beta-Binomial post-count of $B$ conditional on its own baseline logit ($N = 10$) — conditional change, not a raw change score. All three phase transitions are stacked with phase-specific intercepts; the dose enters standardised:

$$
\eta = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{phase}[p]}
     + \beta_G\,G
     + \gamma_{\text{own}}\,\text{logit}(B_{\text{pre}})
     + \gamma_A\,z(\text{age})
     + u_{\text{child}}
     + \beta_{\text{dose}[p]}\,z(\text{attend})
$$

with $\beta_{\text{dose}[p]} = \mu_{\text{dose}} + \sigma_{\text{dose}}\,z_p$ (partial-pooled period slopes).

## Adjustment set (revised DAG)

The focal edge is $\text{dose} \to \text{outcome}$; the back-door set is $\{G, B_{\text{pre}}, A\}$ — $G$ (arm, coded $G = 2-\text{group}$, positive = benefit) is the sole confounder; $B_{\text{pre}}$ is the autoregression / RTM control; age is a maturation precision covariate (no $\text{age} \to \text{dose}$ edge). **Cumulative dose** (`attend_cumul`) is not conditioned on (`IS`-collider descendant, #269). The waitlist controls' period-1 `attend == 0` rows are kept as the zero-dose anchor.

_Adjusted association, not \._ Dose is **not** randomised, so this is an adjusted association; the load-bearing assumption is no $\text{ability} \to \text{dose}$ edge (Frank 2012: the least-able children attended least). Read the dose slope accordingly — the randomised causal effect for blending lives in the ITT suite (`lrp-rli-itt-008`).

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_dose_response.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
